strategy: single universal rule: place bid at T-240 (T-4h), offset 15c below T-20m anchor (bid=max(anchor-15,1))
per_regime_lift: all 36 category x anchor_regime cells show POSITIVE roi lift (+1.3 to +5.9pp). Top: WTA_MAIN r45_54 +5.93, WTA_MAIN r25_34 +5.75, WTA_CHALL r75_84 +4.88, ATP_MAIN r05_14 +4.01. Fill rate rises monotonically with anchor regime (favorites fill far more).

validation_gates.gate1_baseline_reproduction: PASS exact. baseline_total $6,158.20 vs atlas $6,158.20 (ratio 1.0000); capital $70,813.20 exact.
validation_gates.gate3_mode_distribution: PASS. marketable_taker not near-zero (ATP_MAIN r85_94 14%); favorite fill >> underdog fill. NOTE: prompt expected ~50% marketable for favorites and ~mostly-resting for underdogs; actual is 14% marketable on heavy favorites and 96-99% miss on underdogs because the universal 15c offset clamps underdog bids to 1c. This is correct behavior, not a bug.

deviations: SUBSTRATE: atlas exit replay uses spike_perN size_qual_max_250 (>=250ct depth-qualified post-anchor max) + settlement_value as the replay substrate, NOT a g9_trades/post-T-20m trajectory walk. 'exit at +X' triggers iff entry+X <= size_qual_max_250 (interpretation: realized = X above ANY entry, per prompt Step 1/3). Conservative lower bound for entry_minute>20 (pre-T-20m target-hits not added). Substrate question resolved this way; documented before build.

UNDERDOG CLAMP: universal 15c offset clamps underdog bids (anchor<16c) to 1c, so underdog regimes are 96-99% miss_fallback and the lift is favorite-driven. The single-rule policy is effectively a favorite-capture rule; per-regime offsets (Path B v1) needed for underdog harvest.
exit_minute is NULL (size_qual proxy gives the trigger boolean, not the minute).
PRE-REALISM: B25 0.5-0.7x discount NOT applied; these are pre-realism ceiling numbers.
